title: Habenaria volcanica
taxonomy: Habenaria volcanica is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristic orchid-like appearance and growth habits. Within the Orchidaceae family, Habenaria volcanica is placed in the subfamily Orchidoideae, which comprises terrestrial orchids. Further classification places Habenaria volcanica in the tribe Orchideae, a large and diverse group of orchids. It is also classified as belonging to the genus Habenaria, which is a large group of terrestrial orchids found in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Habenaria volcanica, also known as Volcano Orchid, is a species of orchid found in tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria volcanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
